purpose: Guide for authoring custom Synapse commands. Documents the four-tier command resolution hierarchy (Synapse > project > profiles > project_root/_commands), command file templates (standard and overview-style), structural patterns (flags, phased commands, output files, NON-NEGOTIABLE blockquotes), naming conventions, profile templates, duplicate detection, and best practices for writing effective commands.
gotchas: Custom project commands at {project_root}/_commands/ have the LOWEST priority -- they cannot override built-in Synapse or project commands
File name must match command name exactly (eager_dispatch -> eager_dispatch.md)
Use underscores not hyphens (cancel-safe is a rare exception variant)
patterns: Standard template: Purpose / Syntax / Steps / Rules
Overview template: Overview / Usage / Execution Steps / Rules
Phased commands reference sub-files in agent/_commands/{command}_phase.md
Profile template uses Role / Priorities / Constraints / Output Style / Success Criteria / Context Gathering
conventions: Front-load NON-NEGOTIABLE rules in blockquotes for critical commands
Document every flag and argument variant in Syntax section
Include When-to-Use comparison table for overlapping commands
Most commands should be read-only and run in serial mode by default
